summary: API plan covers all 11 API cases (TC-ROLE-001..011) against 7 confirmed endpoints. Auth, fixtures, request/response assertions, and cleanup strategy are concrete and traceable to .aws/data-knowledge.yaml. Five Needs Review items are documented as non-blocking, all related to acceptable codegen-time decisions (helper extension scope, message-string case-insensitivity, update success-path verification, OOB sweep deferral, api ordering stability). No blockers. .aws/data-knowledge.yaml present. Plan is codegen-ready with documented warnings.

needs_review.question: Helper extension to tests/api/helpers/role_api.py adds 6 new functions plus convenience safe_delete_role_by_name. Confirm scope is acceptable (codegen-time additive change, no API surface break).
needs_review.reason: Plan documents this in NR-API-01; .aws/data-knowledge.yaml claims helpers that don't exist on disk. Decision is reasonable given strict additive nature, but worth surfacing.

needs_review.question: TC-ROLE-006 duplicate-name rejection asserts msg.lower() contains 'already exists'. Confirm assertion remains valid if backend localizes error messages.
needs_review.reason: Plan documents this in NR-API-02 with case-insensitive matching; current backend returns English message in this path. Non-blocking.

needs_review.question: TC-ROLE-007 update-desc-persisted: success-path is verified via subsequent GET (not just status 200). Acceptable approach?
needs_review.reason: Plan documents this in NR-API-03; HTTP-readback substitutes for ORM-query assertion since controller is sole DB writer. Non-blocking.

needs_review.question: Out-of-band cleanup sweep for orphan qa-role-* records is deferred to first execution.
needs_review.reason: Plan documents this in NR-API-04; per-test cleanup via fixture is the primary path. Sweep is a backstop. Non-blocking.

needs_review.question: TC-ROLE-011 api_infos rewrite uses tuple-based membership assertion (path,method) rather than ID-based.
needs_review.reason: Plan documents this in NR-API-05; ID stability is not guaranteed across test runs since Api records are seeded by ordinal scan. Tuple is more stable. Non-blocking.
